rulings.Case Title: BORREGAS COURT ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL VS. BOSTON FINANCIAL INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT A ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 15, 2018 line 2. DEFENDANT BFIM-CALIFORNIA ACQUISITION CORP., ERRONEOUSLY, EDISON CAPITAL HOUSING INVESTMENTS, EDISON CAPITAL HOUSING PARTNERS, XI, LLC's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with final leave to amend to address all issues identified in the Order Sustaining Demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint. Additionally, Plaintiff (1) is ordered to either restore the allegation that Plaintiff "attempted" to exercise the option or allege facts showing that removal of this allegation is not a sham pleading; (2) Plaintiff 's opposition is considered as request for leave to amend to add new party and this request is granted. All new allegations must to be in bold font and underlined. Finally, Plaintiff's opposition pages 16-21 are stricken. No order authorizing oversized brief on file. Any oversized brief filed by Plaintiff of Plaintiff's counsel in the future will be stricken in its entirety. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MARY VATINEL VS. HARRY COREN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 15, 2018 line 3. DEFENDANT HARRY COREN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Planitff to allege whether lease is written or oral. If the lease is written, Plaintiff is to attach a copy. If the lease is oral Plaintiff is to state the terms of the lease. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ALEN PETROSYAN VS. AURORA MARTINEZ SANGIL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn For Relief From Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 15, 2018 line 4. DEFENDANTs AURORA SANGIL and SANTIAGO SANGIL's Motion For Relief From Judgment DENIED. Motion is untimely. Advanced Building Maintenance v. State Compensation Insurance Fund (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 1388; CCP 473(b). Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that grounds listed in the Notice give rise to the relief requested. To the extent relief is sought based on fraud (not included in the Notice), moving party failed to meet the burden. Two exhibits attached to the proposed response are not "definite evidence" of fraud. Peterson v. Peterson (1955) 135 Cal.App.2d 812. Defendants' declarations provided with reply are untimely, and in any event, do not support Defendant's claim. The same arguments were previously presented to the Court: "Defendants claims at trial that the certified official records were "fraudulent," but offered no evidence to support their assertion." (March 7, 2018 Statement of Decision). = (501/CFH)
